rulings.Case Title: STATE OF CALIFORNIA EX. REL RON KENNEDY M.D. ET AL VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CONSUMER AFFAIRS
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 20, 2019, Line 2, RESPONDENTS ALEXIS PODESTA, DEAN GRAFILO, DENISE PINES, RONALD LEWIS, MICHELLE BHOLAT, RANDY HAWKINS, HOWARD KRAUSS, KRISTINA LAWSON, SHARON LEVINE, BRENDA SUTTON-WILLIS, DAVID WARMOTH, JAMIE WRIGHT, FELIX YIP, AND PAULETTE ROMERO'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Defendants' demurrer to plaintiff Ron Kennedy, M.D.'s First Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to state a cause of action for injunctive or declaratory relief. (The Court's full tentative ruling has been e-mailed to the parties.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name, and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the tentative ruling verbatim and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Alliance Roofing - Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 20, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT ALLIANCE ROOFING COMPANY, INC.'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Alliance Roofing Company, Inc.'s motion for motion for determination of good faith settlement per Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 877.6 is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown. The settlement satisfies the factors identified in Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488. Intervenor The Travelers Indemnity Company of Connecticut's "statement of position" incorrectly asserts that the good faith determination cannot affect any of its potential claims against Alliance. "A determination by the court that the settlement was made in good faith shall bar any other joint tortfeasor or co-obligor from any further claims against the settling tortfeasor or co-obligor for equitable comparative contribution or partial or comparative indemnity based upon comparative negligence or comparative fault." (Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 877.6(c).) "Because an insurer stands in the shoes of its insured in a subrogation action, the insurer cannot pursue those types of indemnity claims against the settling tortfeasor." (Interstate Fire & Casualty Ins. Co. v. Cleveland Wrecking Co. (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 23, 32.) "However, a good faith settlement does not bar a nonsettling tortfeasor from asserting an indemnification claim against the settling defendants based on an express contract." (Id.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The court will sign the proposed order included with the moving papers. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MINT COLLECTION -410-418 JESSIE STREET CONDOMINIUM VS. FIFTH HISTORIC PROPERTIES, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Good Faith Settlement Order By Defendant And Cross-Defendant Sitco
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 20, 2019, Line 5, CROSS-DEFENDANT ACCESS SYSTEMS & SOLUTIONS, INC.'S Motion For Good Faith Settlement Order By Defendant And Cross-Defendant Sitco. Defendant and cross-defendant Access Systems & Solutions, Inc. Scaffold Inspection and Testing Co. USA Scaffold's ("SITCO") motion for determination of good faith settlement per Code of Civil Procedure Sec. 877.6 is granted. SITCO has submitted evidence that it did not complete its scope of work to design and install a window washing system on the 6 Mint Building as a result of the general contractor's failure to pay; that it would have cost an estimated $25,000 for SITCO to complete that work; that SITCO's limited scope of work bore little relationship to the bulk of plaintiff's estimated cost of removing and replacing the windows at that building; and that other subcontractors with more direct responsibility have already settled with plaintiff for a substantial portion of the total cost of repairing the windows. (McGarry Decl. Paras. 12-14; Carr Decl. Para. 8, Exhs. A, A-1, B, C). SITCO has also submitted evidence that the settlement was reached as a result of arm's length bargaining, with the assistance of a mediator and a special master. (Carr Para. Decl. 9). No evidence of collusion or fraud has been submitted. Under these circumstances, the non-settling defendants have failed to meet their burden to show that the settlement amount is "so far 'out of the ballpark' in relation to [Tech -Bilt] factors as to be inconsistent with the equitable objectives of the statute." (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499-500; see Cahill v. San Diego Gas & Electric Co. (2011) 194 Cal.App.4th 939, 967 [finding a settlement amount of 1% of the claimed damages was within "the ballpark of reasonable settlement amounts."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The court will sign the proposed order submitted by SITCO. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: NAIFEH AZAR VS. MARTHA RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Martha Rodriguez And Pablo Rodriguez'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 20, 2019, Line 4, Defendants Martha Rodriguez And Pablo Rodriguez'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Responses And Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion to compel is unopposed and granted. The plaintiff shall respond without objection and under oath to all discovery referenced in the motion within twenty days of service of a copy of this Order with Notice of Entry. Copies of the discovery pleadings to which a response is required shall be attached as exhibits to the Order. Plaintiff recently became in pro per and the subject discovery was not served directly on him. If he timely responds pursuant to this Order the request for sanctions is denied. If he fails to timely respond a monetary sanction in the amount of $960.00 is awarded to defendant to be paid within forty-five days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GARY STAPLETON VS. BURLINGAME POINT LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 20, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFF GARY STAPLETON's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel production of accident scene photos taken by defendant's employee Vinnie Debarros is granted. Defendant's objection the photographs are attorney-client privileged communications is overruled. Photographs of an accident scene taken by a party and forwarded to an attorney do not themselves meet the definition of a communication. They are physical objects. The party and attorney may have intended the photographs to be privileged but they must first be found to constitute a communication. Transmission to an attorney does not create a privilege if none in fact exists. See: Suezaki v. Superior Court, 58 Cal. 2d 166 (1962) and Magill v. Superior Court, 103 Cal. Rptr. 2d 355 (2001). Defendant's attorney work-product objection is overruled. The photographs are potentially qualified work product which in the discretion of the court may be discoverable. Plaintiff has made the requisite C.C.P. section 2018.030(b) showing denial of the photographs would unfairly prejudice him. He had no opportunity to arrange for accident scene photographs because of his injury. The accident scene conditions were substantially altered shortly after the photographs were taken. The Court sustains defendant's objection to the phrase "each and every" as overbroad language and those words are stricken. The defendant has represented all documents responsive to the categories have been supplied. The defendant shall provide a code complaint amended response reflecting that representation within twenty days from the date of this Order along with the photographs. The written statement from plaintiff to the defendant has been produced and the motion to compel production of said statement is denied as moot. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: 631 FOLSOM STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. FOLSOM VENTURES LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel This Action To Judicial Reference
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Tuesday, August 20, 2019, Line 10, PLAINTIFF 631 FOLSOM STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Compel This Action To Judicial Reference. Plaintiff 631 Folsom Street Owners Association's motion to compel this action to judicial reference is granted. Section 9.1(f) of the parties' CC&Rs, which defendants prepared and executed, expressly states that a judicial referee will resolve the parties' construction defect dispute. (Stander Dec., Paras. 3-6.) This provision is binding on the parties. (See Pinnacle Museum Tower Assn. v. Pinnacle Market Development (US), LLC (2016) 55 Cal.4th 223, 246 [developer could enforce covenant to arbitrate in CC&Rs against homeowners association].) The court does not believe that denial of the motion is necessary to avoid conflicting rulings or prevent judicial waste. (See Tarrant Bell Property, LLC v. Superior Court (2011) 51 Cal.4th 538.) Defendants' cross-complaint for equitable indemnity and contribution and declaratory relief against the manufacturers can be severed and adjudicated after plaintiff's construction defect complaint is resolved. Alternatively, the manufacturer cross-defendants may agree to reference. The court orders litigation of the cross-complaint stayed pending resolution of the complaint. The parties may seek to lift the stay if the manufacturers agree to reference or once the complaint is resolved. The court continues this motion to September 3, 2019. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding the appointment of a referee/s. If the parties cannot agree on a referee, then each party shall submit up to three nominees for appointment by August 29, 2019. (See Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 640(b).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
